sentences.formal: 早上好！你好，我叫David，初次見面，請多多指教。
sentences.colloquial: 早晨！你好呀，我叫David，以後請多多指教。
sentences.en: Good morning! Hi, I'm David — nice to meet you.

sentences.formal: 你好，我是陳小姐，叫我阿珍就可以了。
sentences.colloquial: 你好呀，我係陳小姐，叫我阿珍就得喇。
sentences.en: Hello, I'm Ms Chan — just call me Ah Jan.
Very happy to meet you.

sentences.colloquial: 你係唔係新嚟嘅同事呀？
sentences.en: Are you the new colleague?

sentences.formal: 是的，我剛從倫敦調過來。
sentences.colloquial: 係呀，我啱啱由倫敦調過嚟。
sentences.en: Yes, I just transferred over from London.

sentences.formal: 歡迎你！習慣香港的生活嗎？
sentences.colloquial: 歡迎你呀！香港嘅生活習唔習慣呀？
sentences.en: Welcome! Are you getting used to life in Hong Kong?

sentences.formal: 還可以，就是天氣有點熱。
sentences.en: It's okay — the weather is a bit hot though.

sentences.formal: 慢慢就會習慣了。有需要隨時找我。
sentences.colloquial: 慢慢就會習慣㗎喇。有咩需要隨時搵我。
sentences.en: You'll get used to it. Come find me any time you need something.
Thank you so much!
You're welcome.

title.en: Breakfast at a cha chaan teng

sentences.en: Morning — how many?
Just one, thanks.
Sit over here. What would you like?

sentences.formal: 我要一個A餐，火腿通粉配炒蛋。
sentences.colloquial: 我要個A餐，火腿通粉配炒蛋。
sentences.en: I'll have set A — ham macaroni with scrambled eggs.
And to drink? Cold drinks are two dollars extra.
Hot milk tea, please.
Butter or jam on the toast?
Butter is fine.
Got it, coming right up.
Excuse me — the bill, please!
Thirty-eight dollars in total.
Two of you — do you have a reservation?
No — how long is the wait?

sentences.formal: 大約十五分鐘，請先取票等叫號。
sentences.colloquial: 大概十五分鐘，攞張飛等叫號先啦。
sentences.en: About fifteen minutes — take a ticket and wait for your number.
Fine, we'll wait.
What tea would you like?
Pu-erh, please.

sentences.formal: 這是點心紙，請勾選想吃的交給我。
sentences.colloquial: 呢張係點心紙，剔咗想食嘅畀我啦。
sentences.en: Here's the dim sum sheet — tick what you'd like and hand it back.

sentences.formal: 要一籠蝦餃、一籠燒賣，還有叉燒包。
sentences.colloquial: 要一籠蝦餃、一籠燒賣，仲有叉燒包。
sentences.en: A basket of har gow, a basket of siu mai, and char siu buns.
Anything else?
That's it for now — we'll order more if it's not enough.
Sure.
Where to?

sentences.formal: 麻煩去中環國際金融中心。
sentences.en: To IFC in Central, please.
Western Harbour Tunnel or the Cross-Harbour Tunnel?
Sure — whichever is faster.

sentences.formal: 今天塞車，可能要二十分鐘。
sentences.colloquial: 今日塞車，可能要二十分鐘。
sentences.en: Traffic is bad today — could take twenty minutes.
No problem, I'm not in a hurry.
You can stop at the junction ahead.
Okay — ninety-eight dollars.
Here's a hundred — keep the change.
Thanks a lot!

sentences.formal: 請問，去中環應該在哪個月台上車？
sentences.colloquial: 唔該，去中環應該喺邊個月台上車呀？
sentences.en: Excuse me, which platform should I take for Central?

sentences.formal: 在對面，走樓梯下去就是。
sentences.colloquial: 喺對面，行樓梯落去就係。
sentences.en: It's on the other side — take the stairs down.
Do I need to change trains?
Change to the Island Line at Admiralty.
Roughly how long does it take?
About ten minutes.

sentences.formal: 我的八達通沒有錢了，在哪裡增值？
sentences.colloquial: 我張八達通冇錢喇，喺邊度增值呀？
sentences.en: My Octopus card is out of money — where can I top it up?

sentences.formal: 那邊有增值機，客務中心也可以。
sentences.colloquial: 嗰邊有增值機，去客務中心都得。
sentences.en: There's a top-up machine over there, or the customer service centre.
Got it — thanks so much!
No need to thank me.
Boss, how much is this?
Thirty dollars a catty.

sentences.colloquial: 有啲貴喎，平啲得唔得呀？
sentences.en: That's a bit expensive — can you go lower?
Buy two catties and I'll make it fifty.
Deal — two catties then.
Anything else?
Are these mangoes sweet?
Very sweet — free if they're not!

sentences.formal: 那拿三個。可以用八達通嗎？
sentences.colloquial: 咁攞三個啦。可唔可以嘟八達通呀？
sentences.en: I'll take three then. Can I pay by Octopus?
Sure — seventy-two dollars altogether.
Morning! How was your weekend?

sentences.formal: 不錯，去了遠足，走了麥理浩徑。
sentences.colloquial: 幾好呀，去咗行山，行咗麥理浩徑。
sentences.en: Pretty good — went hiking on the MacLehose Trail.

sentences.formal: 那麼厲害！我整個週末都在加班。
sentences.colloquial: 咁犀利！我成個週末都喺度加班。
sentences.en: Impressive! I spent the whole weekend working overtime.

sentences.colloquial: 辛苦晒。個project趕唔趕得切呀？
sentences.en: Poor you. Will the project make the deadline?

sentences.formal: 應該可以，星期五之前要交給客戶。
sentences.colloquial: 應該得，星期五之前要交畀客戶。
sentences.en: Should be fine — it's due to the client by Friday.
Shout if you need a hand.

sentences.colloquial: 唔該晒！晏晝一齊食飯吖？
sentences.en: Thanks! Lunch together at noon?

sentences.formal: 好啊，樓下那間新開的日本菜怎樣？
sentences.colloquial: 好呀，樓下嗰間新開嘅日本嘢點呀？
sentences.en: Sure — how about that new Japanese place downstairs?
No problem — see you at half past twelve.

sentences.formal: 明天下午三點開會，你有空嗎？
sentences.colloquial: 聽日下晝三點開會，你得唔得閒呀？
sentences.en: Meeting at 3pm tomorrow — are you free?

sentences.formal: 讓我看看日程……三點有另一個會，四點可以嗎？
sentences.colloquial: 等我睇吓個schedule……三點有另一個會，四點得唔得？
sentences.en: Let me check my schedule… I have another meeting at three — does four work?
Sure, let's move it to four.
Which meeting room?

sentences.formal: 十樓的大會議室，我發邀請給你。
sentences.colloquial: 十樓個大會議室，我send個invite畀你。
sentences.en: The big one on the tenth floor — I'll send you an invite.
What's on the agenda?

sentences.formal: 主要討論下一季度的預算。
sentences.colloquial: 主要傾吓下個季度嘅預算。
sentences.en: Mainly next quarter's budget.
Okay, I'll get some materials ready.
Thanks — see you then.

sentences.formal: 這家公司上一季度的業績怎樣？
sentences.colloquial: 呢間公司上個季度嘅業績點呀？
sentences.en: How did this company do last quarter?

sentences.formal: 收入按年增長百分之十五，超出預期。
sentences.colloquial: 收入按年升咗百分之十五，好過預期。
sentences.en: Revenue grew fifteen percent year-on-year — better than expected.
And margins?

sentences.formal: 毛利率維持在百分之四十左右。
sentences.colloquial: Gross margin維持喺百分之四十度。
sentences.en: Gross margin held at around forty percent.

sentences.formal: 管理層對下半年有什麼看法？
sentences.colloquial: 管理層對下半年有咩睇法呀？
sentences.en: What does management think about the second half?

sentences.formal: 他們比較樂觀，計劃擴充東南亞市場。
sentences.colloquial: 佢哋比較樂觀，打算擴充東南亞市場。
sentences.en: They're fairly optimistic — planning to expand in Southeast Asia.
What should we watch on the risk side?

sentences.formal: 主要是成本上升和匯率波動。
sentences.colloquial: 主要係成本上升同埋匯率波動。
sentences.en: Mainly rising costs and currency swings.

sentences.formal: 好，下星期向投資委員會匯報。
sentences.en: Good — we'll take it to the investment committee (IC) next week.
I'll send you the report tonight.

sentences.colloquial: 今晚收工去唔去飲返杯呀？
sentences.en: Drinks after work tonight?
Sure — where should we go?

sentences.formal: 蘭桂坊新開了一間酒吧，聽說不錯。
sentences.colloquial: 蘭桂坊新開咗間酒吧，聽講幾好。
sentences.en: A new bar opened in Lan Kwai Fong — supposedly quite good.
What time?

sentences.colloquial: 七點半得唔得呀？我訂位。
sentences.en: Does half past seven work? I'll book a table.

sentences.formal: 沒問題。要叫其他同事嗎？
sentences.colloquial: 冇問題。使唔使叫埋其他同事呀？
sentences.en: No problem. Should we invite the other colleagues too?

sentences.formal: 好主意，我在群組說一聲。
sentences.en: Good idea — I'll mention it in the group chat.

sentences.formal: 今天我請客，慶祝項目完成！
sentences.en: My treat today — to celebrate finishing the project!
Well, don't mind if I do!
